purpose: Validate governance integrity after the governance recovery exercise. Covers identity validation, glossary validation, routing validation, and lifecycle governance validation.

checks.result: No duplicate module_id values found across 27 modules in module_catalog.json.
checks.evidence: All 27 module_id values are unique: 50000000000000000001 through 50000000000000000026 plus 50000000000000000099 (SHARED_LIBS).

checks.result: No duplicate canonical_symbol values found across 27 modules.
checks.evidence: All 27 canonical symbols are unique in module_catalog.json.

checks.result: All legacy aliases are resolved. 2 legacy aliases exist and both resolve to current canonical symbols.
identifier_map.json description field documents both aliases. The data entries show no orphan references.
checks.evidence: doc_authority.json entry for identifier_map.json: 'Symbol-to-numeric ID map: 27 modules (prefix 5*), 26 steps (prefix 6*), 2 aliases (O2_OMS -> O2_OMS_STATE_MACHINE, O3_PNL_CLASSIFIER -> O3_TRADE_CLOSE_CLASSIFIER)'.

checks.result: module_catalog.json is the sole module identity authority. No parallel identity registries exist (Principle 1 satisfied). The identity_crosswalk.json is correctly classified as migration_reference authority_level 3, not an identity authority.
checks.evidence: doc_authority.json authority_level 1 for module identity: module_catalog.json only.

checks.result: Two modules share the F1 prefix: F1_CONFIG_PREFERENCES (id 50000000000000000001) and F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR (id 50000000000000000025). This is a naming inconsistency, not a functional defect. A future RENAME_SYMBOL MCR targeting F4_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR is documented in module_naming_governance.json.
checks.recommended_action: File MCR: change_type RENAME_SYMBOL, F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR → F4_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR. F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR becomes legacy_alias on the surviving module record.

checks.result: One canonical glossary file: EAFIX_auth_docs/Y_module governance glossary.json (schema_version 1.1.1, 81 terms). No competing authority files.
checks.evidence: glossary_reconciliation_report.json confirms all prior versions (1.1.0, 1.0.0) and working files have zero unique terms not present in the current canonical glossary.

checks.result: No duplicate term names in Y_module governance glossary.json.
Zero definition conflicts found between the deleted v1.1.0 file and the current v1.1.1 file. All 81 terms have identical definitions.
Y_module governance glossary.json is registered in doc_authority.json as classification: canonical, authority_level: 1.

Two working files remain present: module governance glossary bulk terms.json (72 terms) and x_module governance glossary bulk terms.json (77 terms). Both are strict subsets of the canonical 81-term glossary. Classified as archived working artifacts in governance_recovery_inventory.json.
checks.recommended_action: No action required. Files are harmless. May be cleaned up in a future pass if desired.

checks.result: Canonical routing file exists at repository root: eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json.

checks.description: No duplicate routing file in EAFIX_auth_docs
checks.result: The duplicate routing file EAFIX_auth_docs/eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json was deleted in commit 0f2c238. No competing routing authority file exists.
eafix_project_knowledge_reference_routing_instructions.json is registered in doc_authority.json as classification: canonical, authority_level: 1.

module_lifecycle_governance.json created and registered. Contains 9 lifecycle statuses, 17 change types, MCR system design, catalog pass governance, and reconciliation flag queues. Recovered from deleted Mod_refactor_plan.txt.

module_naming_governance.json created and registered. Contains domain prefix table (13 prefixes), ordinal rules, capability name rules, file naming conventions, contract naming rules, numeric ID opacity rules. Recovered from deleted Mod_refactor_plan.txt.

checks.description: All governance files registered in doc_authority
checks.result: All 8 new governance files are registered in doc_authority.json: routing instructions, canonical glossary, lifecycle governance, naming governance, identity crosswalk, recovery inventory, glossary reconciliation report, and this validation report.

Principle 1 satisfied. No canonical_module_registry.json, alternate identity registries, or duplicate module catalogs exist. identity_crosswalk.json is explicitly classified as migration_reference with a note that it must never become an authority source.
checks.evidence: Filesystem scan confirms no duplicate catalog files.

checks.description: Governance knowledge preserved from deleted files
checks.result: All unique governance content from deleted files has been recovered. The deleted module governance glossary.json had zero unique terms. The deleted Mod_refactor_plan.txt had unique lifecycle and naming governance rules — now captured in module_lifecycle_governance.json and module_naming_governance.json.
checks.evidence: governance_recovery_inventory.json contains evidence-based classification for all 7 reviewed files.

criteria.criterion: Module identity has exactly one authoritative source
criteria.evidence: module_catalog.json is the sole identity authority (authority_level 1). identity_crosswalk.json is classified as migration_reference only.
O2_OMS and O3_PNL_CLASSIFIER are recorded as legacy_aliases in module_catalog.json and fully documented in identity_crosswalk.json.
All unique governance content from Mod_refactor_plan.txt is captured in module_lifecycle_governance.json and module_naming_governance.json. Glossary content was already fully preserved.
Duplicate routing file was deleted in commit 0f2c238. Duplicate glossary files are classified as archived working artifacts, not authority sources.
glossary_reconciliation_report.json: 81 active terms, 0 conflicts, 0 unique terms lost from deletions.
module_lifecycle_governance.json created with complete lifecycle rules.
module_naming_governance.json created with complete naming rules.
doc_authority.json: 24 entries including all new governance files.

criteria.criterion: Validation report shows no unresolved identity conflicts
criteria.evidence: No identity conflicts. One pending item: F1 prefix collision (F1_CONFIG_PREFERENCES and F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR share the F1 prefix). Not a functional defect; requires a future RENAME_SYMBOL MCR. All aliases are resolved.

pending_items.description: F1 prefix collision: F1_CONFIG_PREFERENCES and F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR share the F1 prefix.
pending_items.recommended_action: File MCR: RENAME_SYMBOL change_type, renaming F1_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR to F4_FLOW_ORCHESTRATOR. Old symbol becomes legacy_alias.
